page: Vultr voc-g-16c-64gb-320s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server configured with 16 shared vCPUs and 64.0 GB of RAM. Powered by an AMD EPYC processor running at 2.0 GHz, this x86_64 architecture instance features 8 physical cores with 2 threads per core. The processor includes 32 MB of L3 cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 32 KB of L1d cache. It supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 (including AVX512F, AVX512_VNNI, and AVX512_BF16) and cryptographic acceleration via AES and SHA_NI.

Storage is bundled with a local 320 GB NVMe SSD, providing high-speed local disk access. In benchmark evaluations, the server demonstrates top-tier single-core CPU performance in stress-ng tests, placing in the top 10%. Multi-core CPU performance and PassMark CPU scores fall within the average range. Memory performance is a notable strength, with PassMark memory scores ranking in the top 10% and large-block read/write memory bandwidth testing in the top 25%.

The shared CPU allocation introduces potential resource contention tradeoffs compared to dedicated instances, but it offers a cost-efficient profile for workloads requiring high memory capacity and strong single-threaded performance without the premium of dedicated hardware. This server is well-suited for general-purpose workloads, static web serving, database operations, and moderate-scale text processing or small-model LLM inference.
description: Vultr voc-g-16c-64gb-320s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server featuring 16 shared vCPUs and 64.0 GB of RAM on the x86_64 architecture. Powered by an AMD EPYC processor running at 2.0 GHz with 8 physical cores, it includes a 320 GB NVMe SSD for local storage. The hardware delivers top-tier single-core CPU performance and PassMark memory scores, both ranking in the top 10%. Large-block memory bandwidth and single-threaded compression benchmarks also show strong results in the top 25%. While the shared CPU allocation presents a resource-sharing tradeoff, the server provides a cost-efficient balance of memory and compute resources. It is suitable for database operations, static web serving, and moderate machine learning inference workloads.
og_description: Vultr voc-g-16c-64gb-320s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server featuring 16 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and a 320 GB NVMe SSD, delivering top-tier single-core and memory benchmark performance.
meta_description: Vultr voc-g-16c-64gb-320s-amd is an AMD EPYC server with 16 shared vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and 320 GB NVMe storage, optimized for balanced workloads.
tagline: A balanced x86_64 server combining 16 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and high-speed NVMe storage for memory-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 16 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs and 64 GB RAM providing a balanced 1:4 resource ratio
Bundled 320 GB NVMe SSD for fast local storage access
Top-tier single-core CPU and PassMark memory benchmark performance
Support for AVX-512 and cryptographic hardware acceleration
Suitable for database operations, web serving, and moderate LLM inference
